     The Numbers Business Report

Unsung Hero (2024)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $20,335,239Details
International Box Office $858,823
Worldwide Box Office $21,194,062
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $643,526
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $389,522
Total $1,033,048
Further financial details...

Synopsis

David Smallbone moves his family from Down Under to the States, searching for a brighter future after his successful music company collapses. With nothing more than their seven children, suitcases, and their love of music, David and his pregnant wife Helen set out to rebuild their lives. Helen’s faith stands against all odds and inspires her husband and children to hold onto theirs. With their own dreams on hold, David and Helen begin to realize the musical prowess in their children, who would go on to become two of the most successful acts in Inspirational Music history: For King & Country and Rebecca St. James.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,731,539 (38.0% of total gross)
Legs:2.63 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:95.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$6,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,832 opening theaters/2,832 max. theaters, 3.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $21,503,936

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 26th, 2024 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: May 30th, 2024 (Wide) (Australia)
May 30th, 2024 (Limited) (New Zealand)
June 14th, 2024 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
September 26th, 2024 (Limited), released as Notas de Esperanza (Mexico)
Video Release: June 11th, 2024 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for thematic elements.
(Rating bulletin 2808 (cert #54635), 11/8/2023)
Running Time: 112 minutes
Plot point: Immigration, Musicians
Social setting: Christian Contemporary Music, Music Industry
Subgenre: Faith-Based Drama
Target audience: Faith-Based Film
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Kingdom Story Company, Candy Rock, Lionsgate, For King & Country
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

David Smallbone moves his family from Down Under to the States, searching for a brighter future after his successful music company collapses. With nothing more than their seven children, suitcases, and their love of music, David and his pregnant wife Helen set out to rebuild their lives. Helen’s faith stands against all odds and inspires her husband and children to hold onto theirs. With their own dreams on hold, David and Helen begin to realize the musical prowess in their children, who would go on to become two of the most successful acts in Inspirational Music history: For King & Country and Rebecca St. James.
